EnhancedTypeErrorDetail{\n37\t\t\tRawError:    errorStr,\n38\t\t\tLineNumber:  extractLineNumber(errorStr),\n39\t\t\tColumnNumber: extractColumnNumber(errorStr),\n40\t\t\tFieldPath:   extractFieldPath(errorStr),\n41\t\t\tValue:       extractValue(errorStr),\n42\t\t}\n43\t\n44\t\t// Extract type mismatch information\n45\t\tfieldPath, expectedType, actualType := extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr)\n46\t\tif fieldPath != \"\" && detail.FieldPath == \"\" {\n47\t\t\tdetail.FieldPath = fieldPath\n48\t\t}\n49\t\tdetail.Expected = expectedType\n50\t\tdetail.Actual = actualType\n51\t\n52\t\t// Build context message\n53\t\tdetail.Context = buildErrorContext(detail, errorStr)\n54\t\n55\t\treturn detail\n56\t}\n57\t\n58\t// extractTypeMismatchInfo extracts field path, expected type, and actual type from error message.\n59\t//\n60\t// Handles formats like:\n61\t// - \"field server.port type mismatch: expected int, got string\"\n62\t// - \"cannot unmarshal !!str into int\"\n63\t// - \"expected bool, got string\"\n64\t// - \"want float64, got int\"\n65\t//\n66\t// Returns (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType).\n67\tfunc extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr string) (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType string) {\n68\t\terrorStr = strings.TrimSpace(errorStr)\n69\t\n70\t\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch: expected <type>, got <type>\"\n71\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch:\\s*expected\\s+([^\\s,]+),\\s*got\\s+([^\\s.]+)`)\n72\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n73\t\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[3])\n74\t\t}\n75\t\n76\t\t// Pattern 2: \"field <path> cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n77\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n78\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n79\t\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[3]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n80\t\t}\n81\t\n82\t\t// Pattern 3: \"cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n83\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n84\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n85\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n86\t\t}\n87\t\n88\t\t// Pattern 4: \"expected <type>, got <type>\"\n89\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`expected\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n90\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n91\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n92\t\t}\n93\t\n94\t\t// Pattern 5: \"want <type>, got <type>\"\n95\t\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`want\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n96\t\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n97\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n98\t\t}\n99\t\n100\t\t// Pattern 6: \"<type> cannot be converted to <type>\"\n101\t\tre6 := regexp.MustCompile(`(\\S+)\\s+cannot\\s+be\\s+converted\\s+to\\s+(\\S+)`)\n102\t\tif matches := re6.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n103\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n104\t\t}\n105\t\n106\t\t// Pattern 7: \"cannot unmarshal \"<type>\" into \"<type>\"\" (quoted types)\n107\t\tre7 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"`)\n108\t\tif matches := re7.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n109\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn \"\", \"\", \"\"\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// normalizeYAMLType normalizes a YAML/Go type string to a human-readable format.\n116\t//\n117\t// Handles:\n118\t// - YAML type tags: !!str, !!int, !!seq, !!map, !!bool, !!float, !!null\n119\t// - Go basic types: string, int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, float32, float64, bool\n120\t// - Go complex types: []T, *T, map[K]V, chan T, interface{}\n121\t// - Package-qualified types: time.Time, http.Response (strips package name)\n122\t//\n123\t// Examples:\n124\t//   - \"!!str\" → \"string\"\n125\t//   - \"[]string\" → \"array of string\"\n126\t//   - \"*int\" → \"pointer to integer\"\n127\t//   - \"map[string]int\" → \"map[integer]int\"\n128\t//   - \"time.Time\" → \"Time\"\n129\tfunc normalizeYAMLType(typeStr string) string {\n130\t\ttypeStr = strings.TrimSpace(typeStr)\n131\t\n132\t\t// Handle empty string\n133\t\tif typeStr == \"\" {\n134\t\t\treturn \"\"\n135\t\t}\n136\t\n137\t\t// YAML type tags (with !! prefix)\n138\t\tyamlTypes := map[string]string{\n139\t\t\t\"!!str\":   \"string\",\n140\t\t\t\"!!int\":   \"integer\",\n141\t\t\t\"!!float\": \"float\",\n142\t\t\t\"!!bool\":  \"boolean\",\n143\t\t\t\"!!seq\":   \"array\",\n144\t\t\t\"!!map\":   \"object\",\n145\t\t\t\"!!null\":  \"null\",\n146\t\t}\n147\t\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n148\t\t\treturn normalized\n149\t\t}\n150\t\n151\t\t// YAML type tags (without !! prefix - from regex extraction)\n152\t\tyamlTypesNoPrefix := map[string]string{\n153\t\t\t\"str\":   \"string\",\n154\t\t\t\"int\":   \"integer\",\n155\t\t\t\"float\": \"float\",\n156\t\t\t\"bool\":  \"boolean\",\n157\t\t\t\"seq\":   \"array\",\n158\t\t\t\"map\":   \"object\",\n159\t\t\t\"null\":  \"null\",\n160\t\t}\n161\t\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypesNoPrefix[typeStr]; ok {\n162\t\t\treturn normalized\n163\t\t}\n164\t\n165\t\t// Pointer types\n166\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"*\") {\n167\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"**\") {\n168\t\t\t\t// Double pointer - treat as single pointer\n169\t\t\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n170\t\t\t}\n171\t\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[1:])\n172\t\t}\n173\t\n174\t\t// Array/slice types\n175\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"[]\") {\n176\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n177\t\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n178\t\t}\n179\t\n180\t\t// Fixed-size arrays\n181\t\treArray := regexp.MustCompile(`^\\[(\\d+)\\](.+)$`)\n182\t\tif matches := reArray.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n183\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n184\t\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n185\t\t}\n186\t\n187\t\t// Map types - normalize both key and value types\n188\t\treMap := regexp.MustCompile(`^map\\[([^\\]]+)\\](.+)$`)\n189\t\tif matches := reMap.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n190\t\t\t// Normalize both key and value types\n191\t\t\treturn \"map[\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]) + \"]\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n192\t\t}\n193\t\n194\t\t// Channel types - check directional channels first\n195\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"<-chan\") {\n196\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[7:])\n197\t\t\treturn \"receive-only channel of \" + elemType\n198\t\t}\n199\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan<-\") {\n200\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[6:])\n201\t\t\treturn \"send-only channel of \" + elemType\n202\t\t}\n203\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan\") {\n204\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[4:])\n205\t\t\treturn \"channel of \" + elemType\n206\t\t}\n207\t\n208\t\t// Interface types\n209\t\tif typeStr == \"interface{}\" {\n210\t\t\treturn \"interface\"\n211\t\t}\n212\t\n213\t\t// Package-qualified types (e.g., time.Time, http.Response, encoding/json.Marshaler)\n214\t\trePkg := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-z0-9_/]+\\.([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*)$`)\n215\t\tif matches := rePkg.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n216\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n217\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\t// Go basic types - normalize integers\n220\t\tintTypes := map[string]string{\n221\t\t\t\"int\":        \"integer\",\n222\t\t\t\"int8\":       \"integer\",\n223\t\t\t\"int16\":      \"integer\",\n224\t\t\t\"int32\":      \"integer\",\n225\t\t\t\"int64\":      \"integer\",\n226\t\t\t\"uint\":       \"integer\",\n227\t\t\t\"uint8\":      \"unsigned integer\",\n228\t\t\t\"uint16\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n229\t\t\t\"uint32\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n230\t\t\t\"uint64\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n231\t\t\t\"uintptr\":    \"unsigned integer\",\n232\t\t\t\"float32\":    \"float\",\n233\t\t\t\"float64\":    \"float\",\n234\t\t\t\"bool\":       \"boolean\",\n235\t\t\t\"string\":     \"string\",\n236\t\t\t\"rune\":       \"integer\",\n237\t\t\t\"byte\":       \"unsigned integer\",\n238\t\t\t\"complex64\":  \"complex number\",\n239\t\t\t\"complex128\": \"complex number\",\n240\t\t\t\"struct\":     \"object\",\n241\t\t}\n242\t\tif normalized, ok := intTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n243\t\t\treturn normalized\n244\t\t}\n245\t\n246\t\t// Return as-is for unknown types (e.g., custom struct names)\n247\t\treturn typeStr\n248\t}\n249\t\n250\t// extractLineNumber extracts the line number from an error message.\n251\t//\n252\t// Handles formats:\n253\t// - \"line 10: error\"\n254\t// - \"yaml: line 15: error\"\n255\t// - \"error at line 25: ...\"\n256\t// - \"10: error\"\n257\t// - \"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line 30: error\"\n258\t// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n259\t//\n260\t// Returns 0 if no line number is found.\n261\tfunc extractLineNumber(errorStr string) int {\n262\t\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>:\"\n263\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):`)\n264\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n265\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n266\t\t}\n267\t\n268\t\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n269\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+),\\s+column\\s+\\d+`)\n270\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n271\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n272\t\t}\n273\t\n274\t\t// Pattern 3: \"at line <number>\"\n275\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bat\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`)\n276\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n277\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n278\t\t}\n279\t\n280\t\t// Pattern 4: \"<number>:\" at start (after optional prefix)\n281\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\w+:\\s+)?(\\d+):`)\n282\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil && len(matches) > 2 {\n283\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n284\t\t}\n285\t\n286\t\treturn 0\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\t// extractColumnNumber extracts the column number from an error message.\n290\t//\n291\t// Handles formats:\n292\t// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n293\t// - \"line 10:5: error\"\n294\t//\n295\t// Returns 0 if no column number is found.\n296\tfunc extractColumnNumber(errorStr string) int {\n297\t\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n298\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+\\d+,\\s+column\\s+(\\d+)`)\n299\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n300\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n301\t\t}\n302\t\n303\t\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>:<number>\" (note: this might conflict with line:column format)\n304\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):(\\d+):`)\n305\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n306\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n307\t\t}\n308\t\n309\t\treturn 0\n310\t}\n311\t\n312\t// extractFieldPath extracts the field path from an error message.\n313\t//\n314\t// Handles formats:\n315\t// - \"field server.port type mismatch\"\n316\t// - \"field items[0].name cannot unmarshal\"\n317\t// - \"at field server.name\"\n318\t// - \"in field config.key field\"\n319\t//\n320\t// Returns empty string if no field path is found.\n321\tfunc extractFieldPath(errorStr string) string {\n322\t\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch\"\n323\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bfield\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch`)\n324\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n325\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n326\t\t}\n327\t\n328\t\t// Pattern 2: \"field <path> cannot\"\n329\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bfield\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+cannot`)\n330\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n331\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n332\t\t}\n333\t\n334\t\t// Pattern 3: \"at field <path>\"\n335\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bat\\s+field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)`)\n336\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n337\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n338\t\t}\n339\t\n340\t\t// Pattern 4: \"in field <path> field\"\n341\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bin\\s+field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+field`)\n342\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n343\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n344\t\t}\n345\t\n346\t\treturn \"\"\n347\t}\n348\t\n349\t// extractValue extracts the actual value from an error message.\n350\t//\n351\t// Handles formats:\n352\t// - \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\"\n353\t// - \"cannot unmarshal 'world' into int\"\n354\t// - \"cannot unmarshal \"test\" into int\"\n355\t// - \"value: '123' is invalid\"\n356\t// - \"actual value: true\"\n357\t//\n358\t// Returns empty string if no value is found.\n359\tfunc extractValue(errorStr string) string {\n360\t\t// Pattern 1: Backtick-enclosed value (e.g., \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\")\n361\t\t// We need to construct this pattern without using backticks in raw strings\n362\t\tbacktickPattern := regexp.MustCompile(\"!!\\\\w+\\\\s+`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n363\t\tif matches := backtickPattern.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n364\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n365\t\t}\n366\t\n367\t\t// Fallback pattern for backticks without type tag\n368\t\tbacktickFallback := regexp.MustCompile(\"`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n369\t\tif matches := backtickFallback.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n370\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n371\t\t}\n372\t\n373\t\t// Pattern 2: Single-quoted value\n374\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+'([^']+)'\\s+into`)\n375\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n376\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n377\t\t}\n378\t\n379\t\t// Pattern 3: Double-quoted value\n380\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into`)\n381\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n382\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n383\t\t}\n384\t\n385\t\t// Pattern 4: \"value: '<value>'\"\n386\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`value:\\s*['\"]([^'\"]+)['\"]`)\n387\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n388\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n389\t\t}\n390\t\n391\t\t// Pattern 5: \"actual value: <value>\"\n392\t\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`actual\\s+value:\\s*(\\S+)`)\n393\t\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n394\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n395\t\t}\n396\t\n397\t\treturn \"\"\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// inferTypeFromValue infers the type from a string value.\n401\t//\n402\t// Handles:\n403\t// - Booleans: \"true\", \"false\"\n404\t// - Numbers: integers and floats\n405\t// - Strings: quoted or unquoted\n406\t//\n407\t// Returns \"unknown\" if the type cannot be inferred.\n408\tfunc inferTypeFromValue(value string) string {\n409\t\tvalue = strings.TrimSpace(value)\n410\t\n411\t\t// Empty value\n412\t\tif value == \"\" {\n413\t\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n414\t\t}\n415\t\n416\t\t// Boolean\n417\t\tif value == \"true\" || value == \"false\" {\n418\t\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n419\t\t}\n420\t\n421\t\t// Number (integer or float)\n422\t\tre := regexp.MustCompile(`^-?\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$`)\n423\t\tif re.MatchString(value) {\n424\t\t\treturn \"number\"\n425\t\t}\n426\t\n427\t\t// Quoted string (without quotes)\n428\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `\"`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `\"`) {\n429\t\t\treturn \"string\"\n430\t\t}\n431\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `'`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `'`) {\n432\t\t\treturn \"string\"\n433\t\t}\n434\t\n435\t\t// Default to string for anything else\n436\t\treturn \"string\"\n437\t}\n438\t\n439\t// buildErrorContext builds a human-readable error context message.\n440\t//\n441\t// Generates messages like:\n442\t// - \"Unable to convert field 'server.port': expected integer, got string (value: 'abc')\"\n443\t// - \"Type mismatch: expected boolean, got string\"\n444\t// - \"Field 'items[0]' type mismatch: expected string, got integer\"\n445\tfunc buildErrorContext(detail EnhancedTypeErrorDetail, rawError string) string {\n446\t\tvar parts []string\n447\t\n448\t\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n449\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s'\", detail.FieldPath))\n450\t\t}\n451\t\n452\t\tif detail.Expected != \"\" && detail.Actual != \"\" {\n453\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %s, got %s\", detail.Expected, detail.Actual))\n454\t\t}\n455\t\n456\t\tif detail.Value != \"\" {\n457\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"value: '%s'\", detail.Value))\n458\t\t}\n459\t\n460\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n461\t\t\treturn \"Unable to convert value: \" + rawError\n462\t\t}\n463\t\n464\t\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n465\t\t\treturn \"Unable to convert \" + strings.Join(parts, \": \")\n466\t\t}\n467\t\treturn \"Type mismatch: \" + strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n468\t}\n469\t\n470\t// parseIntSafe parses an integer string and returns 0 on failure.\n471\tfunc parseIntSafe(s string) int {\n472\t\tvar result int\n473\t\tfor _, ch := range s {\n474\t\t\tif ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {\n475\t\t\t\tresult = result*10 + int(ch-'0')\n476\t\t\t} else {\n477\t\t\t\treturn 0\n478\t\t\t}\n479\t\t}\n480\t\treturn result\n481\t}\n482\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fd2e7c92-e804-450c-955a-10324bd7c3a3","uuid":"1415e64d-f98b-4da2-ae18-dcca765486d5","timestamp":"2026-07-13T01:20:40.743Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/type_name_extraction.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides type name extraction from yaml.TypeError messages.\n//\n// This module implements logic to extract and normalize type names from\n// yaml.TypeError message strings, handling type names in different positions\n// within error messages.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// EnhancedTypeErrorDetail represents detailed information extracted from a\n// yaml.TypeError message string.\ntype EnhancedTypeErrorDetail struct {\n\tLineNumber  int    // Line number where the error occurred\n\tColumnNumber int   // Column number where the error occurred\n\tFieldPath   string // Field path (e.g., \"server.port\", \"items[0].name\")\n\tExpected    string // Expected type (normalized)\n\tActual      string // Actual type (normalized)\n\tValue       string // Actual value that caused the error\n\tRawError    string // Original error message\n\tContext     string // Generated error context message\n}\n\n// parseTypeErrorString extracts detailed information from a yaml.TypeError message string.\n//\n// This function parses error messages in various formats:\n// - \"line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int\"\n// - \"field server.port type mismatch: expected int, got string\"\n// - \"yaml: line 15: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string\"\n//\n// Returns an EnhancedTypeErrorDetail with all extracted information.\nfunc parseTypeErrorString(errorStr string) EnhancedTypeErrorDetail {\n\tdetail := EnhancedTypeErrorDetail{\n\t\tRawError:    errorStr,\n\t\tLineNumber:  extractLineNumber(errorStr),\n\t\tColumnNumber: extractColumnNumber(errorStr),\n\t\tFieldPath:   extractFieldPath(errorStr),\n\t\tValue:       extractValue(errorStr),\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract type mismatch information\n\tfieldPath, expectedType, actualType := extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr)\n\tif fieldPath != \"\" && detail.FieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\tdetail.FieldPath = fieldPath\n\t}\n\tdetail.Expected = expectedType\n\tdetail.Actual = actualType\n\n\t// Build context message\n\tdetail.Context = buildErrorContext(detail, errorStr)\n\n\treturn detail\n}\n\n// extractTypeMismatchInfo extracts field path, expected type, and actual type from error message.\n//\n// Handles formats like:\n// - \"field server.port type mismatch: expected int, got string\"\n// - \"cannot unmarshal !!str into int\"\n// - \"expected bool, got string\"\n// - \"want float64, got int\"\n//\n// Returns (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType).\nfunc extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr string) (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType string) {\n\terrorStr = strings.TrimSpace(errorStr)\n\n\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch: expected <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch:\\s*expected\\s+([^\\s,]+),\\s*got\\s+([^\\s.]+)`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[3])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"field <path> cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[3]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: \"cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"expected <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`expected\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 5: \"want <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`want\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 6: \"<type> cannot be converted to <type>\"\n\tre6 := regexp.MustCompile(`(\\S+)\\s+cannot\\s+be\\s+converted\\s+to\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re6.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 7: \"cannot unmarshal \"<type>\" into \"<type>\"\" (quoted types)\n\tre7 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"`)\n\tif matches := re7.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\", \"\", \"\"\n}\n\n// normalizeYAMLType normalizes a YAML/Go type string to a human-readable format.\n//\n// Handles:\n// - YAML type tags: !!str, !!int, !!seq, !!map, !!bool, !!float, !!null\n// - Go basic types: string, int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, float32, float64, bool\n// - Go complex types: []T, *T, map[K]V, chan T, interface{}\n// - Package-qualified types: time.Time, http.Response (strips package name)\n//\n// Examples:\n//   - \"!!str\" → \"string\"\n//   - \"[]string\" → \"array of string\"\n//   - \"*int\" → \"pointer to integer\"\n//   - \"map[string]int\" → \"map[integer]int\"\n//   - \"time.Time\" → \"Time\"\nfunc normalizeYAMLType(typeStr string) string {\n\ttypeStr = strings.TrimSpace(typeStr)\n\n\t// Handle empty string\n\tif typeStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// YAML type tags (with !! prefix)\n\tyamlTypes := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"!!str\":   \"string\",\n\t\t\"!!int\":   \"integer\",\n\t\t\"!!float\": \"float\",\n\t\t\"!!bool\":  \"boolean\",\n\t\t\"!!seq\":   \"array\",\n\t\t\"!!map\":   \"object\",\n\t\t\"!!null\":  \"null\",\n\t}\n\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n\t\treturn normalized\n\t}\n\n\t// YAML type tags (without !! prefix - from regex extraction)\n\tyamlTypesNoPrefix := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"str\":   \"string\",\n\t\t\"int\":   \"integer\",\n\t\t\"float\": \"float\",\n\t\t\"bool\":  \"boolean\",\n\t\t\"seq\":   \"array\",\n\t\t\"map\":   \"object\",\n\t\t\"null\":  \"null\",\n\t}\n\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypesNoPrefix[typeStr]; ok {\n\t\treturn normalized\n\t}\n\n\t// Pointer types\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"*\") {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"**\") {\n\t\t\t// Double pointer - treat as single pointer\n\t\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[1:])\n\t}\n\n\t// Array/slice types\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"[]\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\n\t// Fixed-size arrays\n\treArray := regexp.MustCompile(`^\\[(\\d+)\\](.+)$`)\n\tif matches := reArray.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\n\t// Map types - normalize both key and value types\n\treMap := regexp.MustCompile(`^map\\[([^\\]]+)\\](.+)$`)\n\tif matches := reMap.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n\t\t// Normalize both key and value types\n\t\treturn \"map[\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]) + \"]\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Channel types - check directional channels first\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"<-chan\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[7:])\n\t\treturn \"receive-only channel of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan<-\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[6:])\n\t\treturn \"send-only channel of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[4:])\n\t\treturn \"channel of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\n\t// Interface types\n\tif typeStr == \"interface{}\" {\n\t\treturn \"interface\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Package-qualified types (e.g., time.Time, http.Response, encoding/json.Marshaler)\n\trePkg := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-z0-9_/]+\\.([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*)$`)\n\tif matches := rePkg.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Go basic types - normalize integers\n\tintTypes := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"int\":        \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int8\":       \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int16\":      \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int32\":      \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int64\":      \"integer\",\n\t\t\"uint\":       \"integer\",\n\t\t\"uint8\":      \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uint16\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uint32\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uint64\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uintptr\":    \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"float32\":    \"float\",\n\t\t\"float64\":    \"float\",\n\t\t\"bool\":       \"boolean\",\n\t\t\"string\":     \"string\",\n\t\t\"rune\":       \"integer\",\n\t\t\"byte\":       \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"complex64\":  \"complex number\",\n\t\t\"complex128\": \"complex number\",\n\t\t\"struct\":     \"object\",\n\t}\n\tif normalized, ok := intTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n\t\treturn normalized\n\t}\n\n\t// Return as-is for unknown types (e.g., custom struct names)\n\treturn typeStr\n}\n\n// extractLineNumber extracts the line number from an error message.\n//\n// Handles formats:\n// - \"line 10: error\"\n// - \"yaml: line 15: error\"\n// - \"error at line 25: ...\"\n// - \"10: error\"\n// - \"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line 30: error\"\n// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n//\n// Returns 0 if no line number is found.\nfunc extractLineNumber(errorStr string) int {\n\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>:\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+),\\s+column\\s+\\d+`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: \"at line <number>\"\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bat\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"<number>:\" at start (after optional prefix)\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\w+:\\s+)?(\\d+):`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil && len(matches) > 2 {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// extractColumnNumber extracts the column number from an error message.\n//\n// Handles formats:\n// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n// - \"line 10:5: error\"\n//\n// Returns 0 if no column number is found.\nfunc extractColumnNumber(errorStr string) int {\n\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+\\d+,\\s+column\\s+(\\d+)`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>:<number>\" (note: this might conflict with line:column format)\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):(\\d+):`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// extractFieldPath extracts the field path from an error message.\n//\n// Handles formats:\n// - \"field server.port type mismatch\"\n// - \"field items[0].name cannot unmarshal\"\n// - \"at field server.name\"\n// - \"in field config.key field\"\n//\n// Returns empty string if no field path is found.\nfunc extractFieldPath(errorStr string) string {\n\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bfield\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"field <path> cannot\"\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bfield\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+cannot`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: \"at field <path>\"\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bat\\s+field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"in field <path> field\"\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bin\\s+field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+field`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// extractValue extracts the actual value from an error message.\n//\n// Handles formats:\n// - \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\"\n// - \"cannot unmarshal 'world' into int\"\n// - \"cannot unmarshal \"test\" into int\"\n// - \"value: '123' is invalid\"\n// - \"actual value: true\"\n//\n// Returns empty string if no value is found.\nfunc extractValue(errorStr string) string {\n\t// Pattern 1: Backtick-enclosed value (e.g., \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\")\n\t// We need to construct this pattern without using backticks in raw strings\n\tbacktickPattern := regexp.MustCompile(\"!!\\\\w+\\\\s+`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n\tif matches := backtickPattern.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback pattern for backticks without type tag\n\tbacktickFallback := regexp.MustCompile(\"`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n\tif matches := backtickFallback.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: Single-quoted value\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+'([^']+)'\\s+into`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: Double-quoted value\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"value: '<value>'\"\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`value:\\s*['\"]([^'\"]+)['\"]`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 5: \"actual value: <value>\"\n\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`actual\\s+value:\\s*(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// inferTypeFromValue infers the type from a string value.\n//\n// Handles:\n// - Booleans: \"true\", \"false\"\n// - Numbers: integers and floats\n// - Strings: quoted or unquoted\n//\n// Returns \"unknown\" if the type cannot be inferred.\nfunc inferTypeFromValue(value string) string {\n\tvalue = strings.TrimSpace(value)\n\n\t// Empty value\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Boolean\n\tif value == \"true\" || value == \"false\" {\n\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Number (integer or float)\n\tre := regexp.MustCompile(`^-?\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$`)\n\tif re.MatchString(value) {\n\t\treturn \"number\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Quoted string (without quotes)\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `\"`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `\"`) {\n\t\treturn \"string\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `'`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `'`) {\n\t\treturn \"string\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Default to string for anything else\n\treturn \"string\"\n}\n\n// buildErrorContext builds a human-readable error context message.\n//\n// Generates messages like:\n// - \"Unable to convert field 'server.port': expected integer, got string (value: 'abc')\"\n// - \"Type mismatch: expected boolean, got string\"\n// - \"Field 'items[0]' type mismatch: expected string, got integer\"\nfunc buildErrorContext(detail EnhancedTypeErrorDetail, rawError string) string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\n\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s'\", detail.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\tif detail.Expected != \"\" && detail.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %s, got %s\", detail.Expected, detail.Actual))\n\t}\n\n\tif detail.Value != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"value: '%s'\", detail.Value))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"Unable to convert value: \" + rawError\n\t}\n\n\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"Unable to convert \" + strings.Join(parts, \": \")\n\t}\n\treturn \"Type mismatch: \" + strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// parseIntSafe parses an integer string and returns 0 on failure.\nfunc parseIntSafe(s string) int {\n\tvar result int\n\tfor _, ch := range s {\n\t\tif ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {\n\t\t\tresult = result*10 + int(ch-'0')\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn 0\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n","numLines":482,"startLine":1,"totalLines":482}}}

strings.TrimSpace(errorStr)\n54\t\n55\t\t// Pattern 1: \"cannot unmarshal !!<tag> into <type>\"\n56\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!\\w+\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n57\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n58\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n59\t\t}\n60\t\n61\t\t// Pattern 2: \"expected <type>, got <type>\"\n62\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`expected\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+\\S+`)\n63\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n64\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\t// Pattern 3: \"want <type>, got <type>\"\n68\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`want\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+\\S+`)\n69\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n70\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n71\t\t}\n72\t\n73\t\t// Pattern 4: \"<type> cannot be converted to <type>\"\n74\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\S+)\\s+cannot\\s+be\\s+converted\\s+to\\s+\\S+`)\n75\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n76\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n77\t\t}\n78\t\n79\t\t// Pattern 5: Simple type name at start of error: \"<type>: error message\"\n80\t\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\w+(?:\\.\\w+)*):`)\n81\t\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n82\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n83\t\t}\n84\t\n85\t\t// Pattern 6: Quoted type name \"cannot unmarshal \"<type>\" into \"<type>\"\"\n86\t\tre6 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into\\s+\"[^\"]+\"`)\n87\t\tif matches := re6.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n88\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n89\t\t}\n90\t\n91\t\t// Pattern 7: Type name after \"into\": \"...into <type>\"\n92\t\tre7 := regexp.MustCompile(`into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n93\t\tif matches := re7.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n94\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n95\t\t}\n96\t\n97\t\treturn \"\"\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\tfunc parseTypeErrorString(errorStr string) EnhancedTypeErrorDetail {\n101\t\tdetail := EnhancedTypeErrorDetail{\n102\t\t\tRawError:    errorStr,\n103\t\t\tLineNumber:  extractLineNumber(errorStr),\n104\t\t\tColumnNumber: extractColumnNumber(errorStr),\n105\t\t\tFieldPath:   extractFieldPath(errorStr),\n106\t\t\tValue:       extractValue(errorStr),\n107\t\t}\n108\t\n109\t\t// Extract type mismatch information\n110\t\tfieldPath, expectedType, actualType := extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr)\n111\t\tif fieldPath != \"\" && detail.FieldPath == \"\" {\n112\t\t\tdetail.FieldPath = fieldPath\n113\t\t}\n114\t\tdetail.Expected = expectedType\n115\t\tdetail.Actual = actualType\n116\t\n117\t\t// Build context message\n118\t\tdetail.Context = buildErrorContext(detail, errorStr)\n119\t\n120\t\treturn detail\n121\t}\n122\t\n123\t// extractTypeMismatchInfo extracts field path, expected type, and actual type from error message.\n124\t//\n125\t// Handles formats like:\n126\t// - \"field server.port type mismatch: expected int, got string\"\n127\t// - \"cannot unmarshal !!str into int\"\n128\t// - \"expected bool, got string\"\n129\t// - \"want float64, got int\"\n130\t//\n131\t// Returns (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType).\n132\tfunc extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr string) (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType string) {\n133\t\terrorStr = strings.TrimSpace(errorStr)\n134\t\n135\t\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch: expected <type>, got <type>\"\n136\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch:\\s*expected\\s+([^\\s,]+),\\s*got\\s+([^\\s.]+)`)\n137\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n138\t\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[3])\n139\t\t}\n140\t\n141\t\t// Pattern 2: \"field <path> cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n142\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n143\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n144\t\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[3]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n145\t\t}\n146\t\n147\t\t// Pattern 3: \"cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n148\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n149\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n150\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n151\t\t}\n152\t\n153\t\t// Pattern 4: \"expected <type>, got <type>\"\n154\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`expected\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n155\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n156\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n157\t\t}\n158\t\n159\t\t// Pattern 5: \"want <type>, got <type>\"\n160\t\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`want\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n161\t\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n162\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n163\t\t}\n164\t\n165\t\t// Pattern 6: \"<type> cannot be converted to <type>\"\n166\t\tre6 := regexp.MustCompile(`(\\S+)\\s+cannot\\s+be\\s+converted\\s+to\\s+(\\S+)`)\n167\t\tif matches := re6.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n168\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n169\t\t}\n170\t\n171\t\t// Pattern 7: \"cannot unmarshal \"<type>\" into \"<type>\"\" (quoted types)\n172\t\tre7 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"`)\n173\t\tif matches := re7.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n174\t\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n175\t\t}\n176\t\n177\t\treturn \"\", \"\", \"\"\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t// normalizeYAMLType normalizes a YAML/Go type string to a human-readable format.\n181\t//\n182\t// Handles:\n183\t// - YAML type tags: !!str, !!int, !!seq, !!map, !!bool, !!float, !!null\n184\t// - Go basic types: string, int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, float32, float64, bool\n185\t// - Go complex types: []T, *T, map[K]V, chan T, interface{}\n186\t// - Package-qualified types: time.Time, http.Response (strips package name)\n187\t//\n188\t// Examples:\n189\t//   - \"!!str\" → \"string\"\n190\t//   - \"[]string\" → \"array of string\"\n191\t//   - \"*int\" → \"pointer to integer\"\n192\t//   - \"map[string]int\" → \"map[integer]int\"\n193\t//   - \"time.Time\" → \"Time\"\n194\tfunc normalizeYAMLType(typeStr string) string {\n195\t\ttypeStr = strings.TrimSpace(typeStr)\n196\t\n197\t\t// Handle empty string\n198\t\tif typeStr == \"\" {\n199\t\t\treturn \"\"\n200\t\t}\n201\t\n202\t\t// YAML type tags (with !! prefix)\n203\t\tyamlTypes := map[string]string{\n204\t\t\t\"!!str\":   \"string\",\n205\t\t\t\"!!int\":   \"integer\",\n206\t\t\t\"!!float\": \"float\",\n207\t\t\t\"!!bool\":  \"boolean\",\n208\t\t\t\"!!seq\":   \"array\",\n209\t\t\t\"!!map\":   \"object\",\n210\t\t\t\"!!null\":  \"null\",\n211\t\t}\n212\t\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n213\t\t\treturn normalized\n214\t\t}\n215\t\n216\t\t// YAML type tags (without !! prefix - from regex extraction)\n217\t\tyamlTypesNoPrefix := map[string]string{\n218\t\t\t\"str\":   \"string\",\n219\t\t\t\"int\":   \"integer\",\n220\t\t\t\"float\": \"float\",\n221\t\t\t\"bool\":  \"boolean\",\n222\t\t\t\"seq\":   \"array\",\n223\t\t\t\"map\":   \"object\",\n224\t\t\t\"null\":  \"null\",\n225\t\t}\n226\t\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypesNoPrefix[typeStr]; ok {\n227\t\t\treturn normalized\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\t// Pointer types\n231\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"*\") {\n232\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"**\") {\n233\t\t\t\t// Double pointer - treat as single pointer\n234\t\t\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[1:])\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\t// Array/slice types\n240\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"[]\") {\n241\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n242\t\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n243\t\t}\n244\t\n245\t\t// Fixed-size arrays\n246\t\treArray := regexp.MustCompile(`^\\[(\\d+)\\](.+)$`)\n247\t\tif matches := reArray.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n248\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n249\t\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n250\t\t}\n251\t\n252\t\t// Map types - normalize both key and value types\n253\t\treMap := regexp.MustCompile(`^map\\[([^\\]]+)\\](.+)$`)\n254\t\tif matches := reMap.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n255\t\t\t// Normalize both key and value types\n256\t\t\treturn \"map[\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]) + \"]\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n257\t\t}\n258\t\n259\t\t// Channel types - check directional channels first\n260\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"<-chan\") {\n261\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[7:])\n262\t\t\treturn \"receive-only channel of \" + elemType\n263\t\t}\n264\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan<-\") {\n265\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[6:])\n266\t\t\treturn \"send-only channel of \" + elemType\n267\t\t}\n268\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan\") {\n269\t\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[4:])\n270\t\t\treturn \"channel of \" + elemType\n271\t\t}\n272\t\n273\t\t// Interface types\n274\t\tif typeStr == \"interface{}\" {\n275\t\t\treturn \"interface\"\n276\t\t}\n277\t\n278\t\t// Package-qualified types (e.g., time.Time, http.Response, encoding/json.Marshaler)\n279\t\trePkg := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-z0-9_/]+\\.([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*)$`)\n280\t\tif matches := rePkg.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n281\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n282\t\t}\n283\t\n284\t\t// Go basic types - normalize integers\n285\t\tintTypes := map[string]string{\n286\t\t\t\"int\":        \"integer\",\n287\t\t\t\"int8\":       \"integer\",\n288\t\t\t\"int16\":      \"integer\",\n289\t\t\t\"int32\":      \"integer\",\n290\t\t\t\"int64\":      \"integer\",\n291\t\t\t\"uint\":       \"integer\",\n292\t\t\t\"uint8\":      \"unsigned integer\",\n293\t\t\t\"uint16\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n294\t\t\t\"uint32\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n295\t\t\t\"uint64\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n296\t\t\t\"uintptr\":    \"unsigned integer\",\n297\t\t\t\"float32\":    \"float\",\n298\t\t\t\"float64\":    \"float\",\n299\t\t\t\"bool\":       \"boolean\",\n300\t\t\t\"string\":     \"string\",\n301\t\t\t\"rune\":       \"integer\",\n302\t\t\t\"byte\":       \"unsigned integer\",\n303\t\t\t\"complex64\":  \"complex number\",\n304\t\t\t\"complex128\": \"complex number\",\n305\t\t\t\"struct\":     \"object\",\n306\t\t}\n307\t\tif normalized, ok := intTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n308\t\t\treturn normalized\n309\t\t}\n310\t\n311\t\t// Return as-is for unknown types (e.g., custom struct names)\n312\t\treturn typeStr\n313\t}\n314\t\n315\t// extractLineNumber extracts the line number from an error message.\n316\t//\n317\t// Handles formats:\n318\t// - \"line 10: error\"\n319\t// - \"yaml: line 15: error\"\n320\t// - \"error at line 25: ...\"\n321\t// - \"10: error\"\n322\t// - \"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line 30: error\"\n323\t// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n324\t//\n325\t// Returns 0 if no line number is found.\n326\tfunc extractLineNumber(errorStr string) int {\n327\t\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>:\"\n328\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):`)\n329\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n330\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n331\t\t}\n332\t\n333\t\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n334\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+),\\s+column\\s+\\d+`)\n335\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n336\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n337\t\t}\n338\t\n339\t\t// Pattern 3: \"at line <number>\"\n340\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bat\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`)\n341\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n342\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n343\t\t}\n344\t\n345\t\t// Pattern 4: \"<number>:\" at start (after optional prefix)\n346\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\w+:\\s+)?(\\d+):`)\n347\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil && len(matches) > 2 {\n348\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n349\t\t}\n350\t\n351\t\treturn 0\n352\t}\n353\t\n354\t// extractColumnNumber extracts the column number from an error message.\n355\t//\n356\t// Handles formats:\n357\t// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n358\t// - \"line 10:5: error\"\n359\t//\n360\t// Returns 0 if no column number is found.\n361\tfunc extractColumnNumber(errorStr string) int {\n362\t\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n363\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+\\d+,\\s+column\\s+(\\d+)`)\n364\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n365\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n366\t\t}\n367\t\n368\t\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>:<number>\" (note: this might conflict with line:column format)\n369\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):(\\d+):`)\n370\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n371\t\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n372\t\t}\n373\t\n374\t\treturn 0\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t// extractFieldPath extracts the field path from an error message.\n378\t//\n379\t// Handles formats:\n380\t// - \"field server.port type mismatch\"\n381\t// - \"field items[0].name cannot unmarshal\"\n382\t// - \"at field server.name\"\n383\t// - \"in field config.key field\"\n384\t//\n385\t// Returns empty string if no field path is found.\n386\tfunc extractFieldPath(errorStr string) string {\n387\t\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch\"\n388\t\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bfield\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch`)\n389\t\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n390\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n391\t\t}\n392\t\n393\t\t// Pattern 2: \"field <path> cannot\"\n394\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bfield\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+cannot`)\n395\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n396\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n397\t\t}\n398\t\n399\t\t// Pattern 3: \"at field <path>\"\n400\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bat\\s+field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)`)\n401\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n402\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n403\t\t}\n404\t\n405\t\t// Pattern 4: \"in field <path> field\"\n406\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bin\\s+field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+field`)\n407\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n408\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n409\t\t}\n410\t\n411\t\treturn \"\"\n412\t}\n413\t\n414\t// extractValue extracts the actual value from an error message.\n415\t//\n416\t// Handles formats:\n417\t// - \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\"\n418\t// - \"cannot unmarshal 'world' into int\"\n419\t// - \"cannot unmarshal \"test\" into int\"\n420\t// - \"value: '123' is invalid\"\n421\t// - \"actual value: true\"\n422\t//\n423\t// Returns empty string if no value is found.\n424\tfunc extractValue(errorStr string) string {\n425\t\t// Pattern 1: Backtick-enclosed value (e.g., \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\")\n426\t\t// We need to construct this pattern without using backticks in raw strings\n427\t\tbacktickPattern := regexp.MustCompile(\"!!\\\\w+\\\\s+`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n428\t\tif matches := backtickPattern.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n429\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n430\t\t}\n431\t\n432\t\t// Fallback pattern for backticks without type tag\n433\t\tbacktickFallback := regexp.MustCompile(\"`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n434\t\tif matches := backtickFallback.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n435\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n436\t\t}\n437\t\n438\t\t// Pattern 2: Single-quoted value\n439\t\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+'([^']+)'\\s+into`)\n440\t\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n441\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n442\t\t}\n443\t\n444\t\t// Pattern 3: Double-quoted value\n445\t\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into`)\n446\t\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n447\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n448\t\t}\n449\t\n450\t\t// Pattern 4: \"value: '<value>'\"\n451\t\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`value:\\s*['\"]([^'\"]+)['\"]`)\n452\t\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n453\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n454\t\t}\n455\t\n456\t\t// Pattern 5: \"actual value: <value>\"\n457\t\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`actual\\s+value:\\s*(\\S+)`)\n458\t\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n459\t\t\treturn matches[1]\n460\t\t}\n461\t\n462\t\treturn \"\"\n463\t}\n464\t\n465\t// inferTypeFromValue infers the type from a string value.\n466\t//\n467\t// Handles:\n468\t// - Booleans: \"true\", \"false\"\n469\t// - Numbers: integers and floats\n470\t// - Strings: quoted or unquoted\n471\t//\n472\t// Returns \"unknown\" if the type cannot be inferred.\n473\tfunc inferTypeFromValue(value string) string {\n474\t\tvalue = strings.TrimSpace(value)\n475\t\n476\t\t// Empty value\n477\t\tif value == \"\" {\n478\t\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n479\t\t}\n480\t\n481\t\t// Boolean\n482\t\tif value == \"true\" || value == \"false\" {\n483\t\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\t// Number (integer or float)\n487\t\tre := regexp.MustCompile(`^-?\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$`)\n488\t\tif re.MatchString(value) {\n489\t\t\treturn \"number\"\n490\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\t// Quoted string (without quotes)\n493\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `\"`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `\"`) {\n494\t\t\treturn \"string\"\n495\t\t}\n496\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `'`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `'`) {\n497\t\t\treturn \"string\"\n498\t\t}\n499\t\n500\t\t// Default to string for anything else\n501\t\treturn \"string\"\n502\t}\n503\t\n504\t// buildErrorContext builds a human-readable error context message.\n505\t//\n506\t// Generates messages like:\n507\t// - \"Unable to convert field 'server.port': expected integer, got string (value: 'abc')\"\n508\t// - \"Type mismatch: expected boolean, got string\"\n509\t// - \"Field 'items[0]' type mismatch: expected string, got integer\"\n510\tfunc buildErrorContext(detail EnhancedTypeErrorDetail, rawError string) string {\n511\t\tvar parts []string\n512\t\n513\t\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n514\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s'\", detail.FieldPath))\n515\t\t}\n516\t\n517\t\tif detail.Expected != \"\" && detail.Actual != \"\" {\n518\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %s, got %s\", detail.Expected, detail.Actual))\n519\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\tif detail.Value != \"\" {\n522\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"value: '%s'\", detail.Value))\n523\t\t}\n524\t\n525\t\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n526\t\t\treturn \"Unable to convert value: \" + rawError\n527\t\t}\n528\t\n529\t\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n530\t\t\treturn \"Unable to convert \" + strings.Join(parts, \": \")\n531\t\t}\n532\t\treturn \"Type mismatch: \" + strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n533\t}\n534\t\n535\t// parseIntSafe parses an integer string and returns 0 on failure.\n536\tfunc parseIntSafe(s string) int {\n537\t\tvar result int\n538\t\tfor _, ch := range s {\n539\t\t\tif ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {\n540\t\t\t\tresult = result*10 + int(ch-'0')\n541\t\t\t} else {\n542\t\t\t\treturn 0\n543\t\t\t}\n544\t\t}\n545\t\treturn Package yamlutil provides type name extraction from yaml.TypeError messages.\n//\n// This module implements logic to extract and normalize type names from\n// yaml.TypeError message strings, handling type names in different positions\n// within error messages.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"regexp\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// EnhancedTypeErrorDetail represents detailed information extracted from a\n// yaml.TypeError message string.\ntype EnhancedTypeErrorDetail struct {\n\tLineNumber  int    // Line number where the error occurred\n\tColumnNumber int   // Column number where the error occurred\n\tFieldPath   string // Field path (e.g., \"server.port\", \"items[0].name\")\n\tExpected    string // Expected type (normalized)\n\tActual      string // Actual type (normalized)\n\tValue       string // Actual value that caused the error\n\tRawError    string // Original error message\n\tContext     string // Generated error context message\n}\n\n// parseTypeErrorString extracts detailed information from a yaml.TypeError message string.\n//\n// This function parses error messages in various formats:\n// - \"line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int\"\n// - \"field server.port type mismatch: expected int, got string\"\n// - \"yaml: line 15: cannot unmarshal !!seq into []string\"\n//\n// Returns an EnhancedTypeErrorDetail with all extracted information.\n// extractTypeName extracts the first type name from a yaml.TypeError message string.\n//\n// This function uses basic regex patterns to extract type names from error messages,\n// handling simple cases where type names appear in common positions.\n//\n// Patterns matched:\n// - \"cannot unmarshal !!<tag> into <type>\" → extracts \"<type>\"\n// - \"expected <type>, got <type>\" → extracts first \"<type>\"\n// - \"want <type>, got <type>\" → extracts first \"<type>\"\n// - \"<type> cannot be converted to <type>\" → extracts first \"<type>\"\n// - Simple type names at start: \"<type>: error\" → extracts \"<type>\"\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   errorStr - The error message string to parse\n//\n// Returns:\n//   The first matched type name, or empty string if no match found\nfunc extractTypeName(errorStr string) string {\n\terrorStr = strings.TrimSpace(errorStr)\n\n\t// Pattern 1: \"cannot unmarshal !!<tag> into <type>\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!\\w+\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"expected <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`expected\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+\\S+`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: \"want <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`want\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+\\S+`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"<type> cannot be converted to <type>\"\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\S+)\\s+cannot\\s+be\\s+converted\\s+to\\s+\\S+`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 5: Simple type name at start of error: \"<type>: error message\"\n\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\w+(?:\\.\\w+)*):`)\n\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 6: Quoted type name \"cannot unmarshal \"<type>\" into \"<type>\"\"\n\tre6 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into\\s+\"[^\"]+\"`)\n\tif matches := re6.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 7: Type name after \"into\": \"...into <type>\"\n\tre7 := regexp.MustCompile(`into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re7.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\nfunc parseTypeErrorString(errorStr string) EnhancedTypeErrorDetail {\n\tdetail := EnhancedTypeErrorDetail{\n\t\tRawError:    errorStr,\n\t\tLineNumber:  extractLineNumber(errorStr),\n\t\tColumnNumber: extractColumnNumber(errorStr),\n\t\tFieldPath:   extractFieldPath(errorStr),\n\t\tValue:       extractValue(errorStr),\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract type mismatch information\n\tfieldPath, expectedType, actualType := extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr)\n\tif fieldPath != \"\" && detail.FieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\tdetail.FieldPath = fieldPath\n\t}\n\tdetail.Expected = expectedType\n\tdetail.Actual = actualType\n\n\t// Build context message\n\tdetail.Context = buildErrorContext(detail, errorStr)\n\n\treturn detail\n}\n\n// extractTypeMismatchInfo extracts field path, expected type, and actual type from error message.\n//\n// Handles formats like:\n// - \"field server.port type mismatch: expected int, got string\"\n// - \"cannot unmarshal !!str into int\"\n// - \"expected bool, got string\"\n// - \"want float64, got int\"\n//\n// Returns (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType).\nfunc extractTypeMismatchInfo(errorStr string) (fieldPath, expectedType, actualType string) {\n\terrorStr = strings.TrimSpace(errorStr)\n\n\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch: expected <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch:\\s*expected\\s+([^\\s,]+),\\s*got\\s+([^\\s.]+)`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[3])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"field <path> cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`field\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1], normalizeYAMLType(matches[3]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: \"cannot unmarshal !!<type> into <type>\"\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+!!(\\w+)\\s+into\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"expected <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`expected\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 5: \"want <type>, got <type>\"\n\tre5 := regexp.MustCompile(`want\\s+(\\S+),\\s*got\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re5.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 6: \"<type> cannot be converted to <type>\"\n\tre6 := regexp.MustCompile(`(\\S+)\\s+cannot\\s+be\\s+converted\\s+to\\s+(\\S+)`)\n\tif matches := re6.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 7: \"cannot unmarshal \"<type>\" into \"<type>\"\" (quoted types)\n\tre7 := regexp.MustCompile(`cannot\\s+unmarshal\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"`)\n\tif matches := re7.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", normalizeYAMLType(matches[2]), normalizeYAMLType(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\", \"\", \"\"\n}\n\n// normalizeYAMLType normalizes a YAML/Go type string to a human-readable format.\n//\n// Handles:\n// - YAML type tags: !!str, !!int, !!seq, !!map, !!bool, !!float, !!null\n// - Go basic types: string, int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, float32, float64, bool\n// - Go complex types: []T, *T, map[K]V, chan T, interface{}\n// - Package-qualified types: time.Time, http.Response (strips package name)\n//\n// Examples:\n//   - \"!!str\" → \"string\"\n//   - \"[]string\" → \"array of string\"\n//   - \"*int\" → \"pointer to integer\"\n//   - \"map[string]int\" → \"map[integer]int\"\n//   - \"time.Time\" → \"Time\"\nfunc normalizeYAMLType(typeStr string) string {\n\ttypeStr = strings.TrimSpace(typeStr)\n\n\t// Handle empty string\n\tif typeStr == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// YAML type tags (with !! prefix)\n\tyamlTypes := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"!!str\":   \"string\",\n\t\t\"!!int\":   \"integer\",\n\t\t\"!!float\": \"float\",\n\t\t\"!!bool\":  \"boolean\",\n\t\t\"!!seq\":   \"array\",\n\t\t\"!!map\":   \"object\",\n\t\t\"!!null\":  \"null\",\n\t}\n\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n\t\treturn normalized\n\t}\n\n\t// YAML type tags (without !! prefix - from regex extraction)\n\tyamlTypesNoPrefix := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"str\":   \"string\",\n\t\t\"int\":   \"integer\",\n\t\t\"float\": \"float\",\n\t\t\"bool\":  \"boolean\",\n\t\t\"seq\":   \"array\",\n\t\t\"map\":   \"object\",\n\t\t\"null\":  \"null\",\n\t}\n\tif normalized, ok := yamlTypesNoPrefix[typeStr]; ok {\n\t\treturn normalized\n\t}\n\n\t// Pointer types\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"*\") {\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"**\") {\n\t\t\t// Double pointer - treat as single pointer\n\t\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"pointer to \" + normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[1:])\n\t}\n\n\t// Array/slice types\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"[]\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[2:])\n\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\n\t// Fixed-size arrays\n\treArray := regexp.MustCompile(`^\\[(\\d+)\\](.+)$`)\n\tif matches := reArray.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t\treturn \"array of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\n\t// Map types - normalize both key and value types\n\treMap := regexp.MustCompile(`^map\\[([^\\]]+)\\](.+)$`)\n\tif matches := reMap.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n\t\t// Normalize both key and value types\n\t\treturn \"map[\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[1]) + \"]\" + normalizeYAMLType(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\t// Channel types - check directional channels first\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"<-chan\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[7:])\n\t\treturn \"receive-only channel of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan<-\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[6:])\n\t\treturn \"send-only channel of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(typeStr, \"chan\") {\n\t\telemType := normalizeYAMLType(typeStr[4:])\n\t\treturn \"channel of \" + elemType\n\t}\n\n\t// Interface types\n\tif typeStr == \"interface{}\" {\n\t\treturn \"interface\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Package-qualified types (e.g., time.Time, http.Response, encoding/json.Marshaler)\n\trePkg := regexp.MustCompile(`^[a-z0-9_/]+\\.([A-Z][a-zA-Z0-9]*)$`)\n\tif matches := rePkg.FindStringSubmatch(typeStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Go basic types - normalize integers\n\tintTypes := map[string]string{\n\t\t\"int\":        \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int8\":       \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int16\":      \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int32\":      \"integer\",\n\t\t\"int64\":      \"integer\",\n\t\t\"uint\":       \"integer\",\n\t\t\"uint8\":      \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uint16\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uint32\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uint64\":     \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"uintptr\":    \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"float32\":    \"float\",\n\t\t\"float64\":    \"float\",\n\t\t\"bool\":       \"boolean\",\n\t\t\"string\":     \"string\",\n\t\t\"rune\":       \"integer\",\n\t\t\"byte\":       \"unsigned integer\",\n\t\t\"complex64\":  \"complex number\",\n\t\t\"complex128\": \"complex number\",\n\t\t\"struct\":     \"object\",\n\t}\n\tif normalized, ok := intTypes[typeStr]; ok {\n\t\treturn normalized\n\t}\n\n\t// Return as-is for unknown types (e.g., custom struct names)\n\treturn typeStr\n}\n\n// extractLineNumber extracts the line number from an error message.\n//\n// Handles formats:\n// - \"line 10: error\"\n// - \"yaml: line 15: error\"\n// - \"error at line 25: ...\"\n// - \"10: error\"\n// - \"error converting YAML to JSON: yaml: line 30: error\"\n// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n//\n// Returns 0 if no line number is found.\nfunc extractLineNumber(errorStr string) int {\n\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>:\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+),\\s+column\\s+\\d+`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: \"at line <number>\"\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bat\\s+line\\s+(\\d+)`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"<number>:\" at start (after optional prefix)\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`^(\\w+:\\s+)?(\\d+):`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil && len(matches) > 2 {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// extractColumnNumber extracts the column number from an error message.\n//\n// Handles formats:\n// - \"line 10, column 5: error\"\n// - \"line 10:5: error\"\n//\n// Returns 0 if no column number is found.\nfunc extractColumnNumber(errorStr string) int {\n\t// Pattern 1: \"line <number>, column <number>\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+\\d+,\\s+column\\s+(\\d+)`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: \"line <number>:<number>\" (note: this might conflict with line:column format)\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bline\\s+(\\d+):(\\d+):`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn parseIntSafe(matches[2])\n\t}\n\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// extractFieldPath \"in field config.key field\"\n//\n// Returns empty string if no field path is found.\nfunc extractFieldPath(errorStr string) string {\n\t// Pattern 1: \"field <path> type mismatch\"\n\tre1 := regexp.MustCompile(`\\bfield\\s+([^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?(?:\\.[^\\s]+(?:\\[[^\\]]+\\])?)*)\\s+type\\s+mismatch`)\n\tif matches := re1.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: \"at field <path>\"\n\tre3 := \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\"\n// - \"cannot unmarshal 'world' into int\"\n// - \"cannot unmarshal \"test\" into int\"\n// - \"value: '123' is invalid\"\n// - \"actual value: true\"\n//\n// Returns empty string if no value is found.\nfunc extractValue(errorStr string) string {\n\t// Pattern 1: Backtick-enclosed value (e.g., \"cannot unmarshal !!str `hello` into int\")\n\t// We need to construct this pattern without using backticks in raw strings\n\tbacktickPattern := regexp.MustCompile(\"!!\\\\w+\\\\s+`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n\tif matches := backtickPattern.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Fallback pattern for backticks without type tag\n\tbacktickFallback := regexp.MustCompile(\"`([^`]+)`\\\\s+into\")\n\tif matches := backtickFallback.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 2: Single-quoted value\n\tre2 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+'([^']+)'\\s+into`)\n\tif matches := re2.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 3: Double-quoted value\n\tre3 := regexp.MustCompile(`(?:unmarshal|marshal)\\s+\"([^\"]+)\"\\s+into`)\n\tif matches := re3.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 4: \"value: '<value>'\"\n\tre4 := regexp.MustCompile(`value:\\s*['\"]([^'\"]+)['\"]`)\n\tif matches := re4.FindStringSubmatch(errorStr); matches != nil {\n\t\treturn matches[1]\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 5: \"\"\n}\n\n// inferTypeFromValue infers the type from a string value.\n//\n// Handles:\n// - Booleans: \"true\", \"false\"\n// - Numbers: integers and floats\n// - Strings: quoted or unquoted\n//\n// Returns \"unknown\" if the type cannot be inferred.\nfunc inferTypeFromValue(value string) string {\n\tvalue = strings.TrimSpace(value)\n\n\t// Empty value\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"unknown\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Boolean\n\tif value == \"true\" || value == \"false\" {\n\t\treturn \"boolean\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Number (integer or float)\n\tre := regexp.MustCompile(`^-?\\d+(\\.\\d+)?$`)\n\tif re.MatchString(value) {\n\t\treturn \"number\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Quoted string (without quotes)\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `\"`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `\"`) {\n\t\treturn \"string\"\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(value, `'`) && strings.HasSuffix(value, `'`) {\n\t\treturn \"string\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Default to string for anything else\n\treturn \"string\"\n}\n\n// buildErrorContext builds a human-readable error context message.\n//\n// Generates messages like:\n// - \"Unable to convert field 'server.port': expected integer, got string (value: 'abc')\"\n// - \"Type mismatch: expected boolean, got string\"\n// - \"Field 'items[0]' type mismatch: expected string, got integer\"\nfunc buildErrorContext(detail EnhancedTypeErrorDetail, rawError string) string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\n\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"field '%s'\", detail.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\tif detail.Expected != \"\" && detail.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %s, got %s\", detail.Expected, detail.Actual))\n\t}\n\n\tif detail.Value != \"\" {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"value: '%s'\", detail.Value))\n\t}\n\n\tif len(parts) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"Unable to convert value: \" + rawError\n\t}\n\n\tif detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"Unable to convert \" + strings.Join(parts, \": \")\n\t}\n\treturn \"Type mismatch: \" + strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// parseIntSafe parses an integer string and returns 0 on failure.\nfunc parseIntSafe(s string) int {\n\tvar result int\n\tfor _, ch := range s {\n\t\tif ch >= '0' && ch <= '9' {\n\t\t\tresult = result*10 + int(ch-'0')\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\treturn 0\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n","numLines":547,"startLine":1,"totalLines":547}}}

It serves as the foundation for type name extraction.\n//\n// Supported patterns:\n// - YAML type tags: !!str, !!int, !!seq, !!map, !!bool, !!float, !!null\n// - Go basic types: string, int, int8, int16, int32, int64, uint, float32, float64, bool\n// - Go slice types: []string, []int, etc.\n// - Go pointer types: *string, *int, etc.\n// - Go map types: map[string]int, etc.\n//\n// The function searches the error message and returns the first matched type name,\n// or an empty string if no match is found.\n//\n// Examples:\n// - \"line 10: cannot unmarshal !!str into int\" → returns \"str\"\n// - \"cannot unmarshal !!int into string\" → returns \"int\"\n// - \"expected []string, got int\" → returns \"[]string\"\n// - \"field type: *bool expected\" → returns \"*bool\"\n// - \"map cannot unmarshal\" → returns \"\"\n//\n// Returns empty string when no match is found.\nfunc ExtractTypeNameBasic(errorMsg string) string {\n\terrorMsg = strings.TrimSpace(errorMsg)\n\tif errorMsg == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Pattern 1: YAML type tags (e.g., !!str, !!int, !!seq)\n\t// These are the most common and should be checked first\n\tyamlTypeTagRegex := regexp.MustCompile(`!!(\\w+)`)\n\tif matches := yamlTypeTagRegex.FindStringSubmatch(errorMsg);
